title: phoradendron rugosum
intro: Phoradendron rugosum, also known as the roughleaf mistletoe, is a type of hemiparasitic plant that grows on the branches of trees, obtaining its nutrients by tapping into the host plant's vascular system. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Phoradendron rugosum is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
